pi_items.source: setup_task.sh seed_data array - counted actual types: Tomato (4), Lettuce (3), Bean (4), Pepper (3), Squash (2), Herb (3), Zucchini (1)
Web search results from multiple gardening sources indicate tomato seeds remain viable for 4-7 years under proper storage, with some sources reporting up to 10 years at declining germination rates

Web search indicates lettuce seeds viable for 5-6 years under proper storage. Task value of 1-3 years is conservative/incorrect. Sources cite up to 6 years for standard lettuce seeds (pelleted seeds are shorter at ~1 year)
Web search indicates bean seeds viable for 3 years minimum, up to 5 years in ideal storage conditions
Web search confirms squash seeds viable for 4-6 years under proper storage (some sources report 3 years as minimum)
Web search confirms pepper seeds viable for 2-4 years, with potential extension to 5 years in optimal conditions
Web search indicates herbs typically 2-6 years but can fail after 1 year due to thin seed coats. Task value is conservative but within lower range

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic seed viability task using 20 seed entries created in ODS format with two sheets. Verified facts: 20 seeds total, collection dates from 2018-04-05 to 2024-03-22, medium difficulty, 180-second timeout, 50 max steps. The task claims reference data for 6 seed types (Tomato, Lettuce, Bean, Squash, Pepper, Herb), but the actual inventory includes 'Zucchini' which is NOT in the reference sheet—this will cause VLOOKUP failures for the Zucchini seed (S004 'Black Beauty'). Seed viability ranges are mostly accurate: Tomato 4-6 years (verified as 4-7 years), Bean 3-4 years (verified), Squash 4-6 years (verified), Pepper 2-4 years (verified), Herb 1-2 years (verified as conservative estimate of 2-6 year range). CRITICAL ISSUE: Lettuce viability is listed as 1-3 years in the reference data, but actual research indicates 5-6 years—this is a significant contradiction that affects the task's accuracy. Data is entirely synthetic, created by setup script.
